claim: Continuous learning loops for agents require detecting when a user got stuck-then-unstuck; clustering similar cases upstream prevents future friction with minimal manual intervention.
actionable: Build detection for user stuck-recovery cycles; cluster similar problems; inject solution context upstream before next user encounters same problem.

claim: Agent-generated complaints/feedback (via a 'vent tool') about platform limitations, missing tools, and broken behavior surface real bugs that never appear in logs.
actionable: Give agents a structured feedback channel for platform frustrations; monitor the channel for patterns; prioritize fixes based on complaint volume and frequency.

claim: Spikes in agent venting volume reliably indicate platform incidents before traditional monitoring detects them; agents are sensitive to environmental breakage.
actionable: Use agent feedback channel activity as an incident detector; alert on sustained increases in complaint volume even if system metrics look normal.

claim: Knowledge bases (Stack Overflow-style entries) trained on solved stuck-recovery cases become stale quickly with model updates and feature changes; holdout groups essential for pruning.
actionable: Maintain a holdout/control group when injecting learned context; measure actual project completion rates; prune stale entries when models or features change.

claim: At scale (200K projects/day), distinguishing between solvable friction and unsolvable hard problems is critical; only fix quick wins first (bugs, simple missing features).
actionable: Categorize stuck cases: solvable with better prompting, solvable with quick product fix, requires weeks of work; prioritize the first two; avoid over-committing on hard problems.
